media.notes: The paper names this 'DSM 141c medium' (ref 43, Liang 2022, M. orientis sp. nov.). DSMZ Medium 141c is the 'Methanococcoides medium' and contains trimethylammonium chloride as the native substrate - an exact match to the M. orientis TMA substrate described. Full recipe recovered from the DSMZ 141c PDF, including the Modified Wolin's mineral solution (12 trace metals + NTA) and Wolin's vitamin solution 10x (10 vitamins). For this experiment the growth substrate trimethylamine (and the 1 g/L base Na-acetate) were omitted ('minus acetate or trimethylamine'), methane was the electron donor (headspace), and humic acids (0.22 g/L) were the electron acceptor; anaerobic N2/CO2 80/20 gas phase, cysteine+Na2S reductant, pH 7.2-7.4, 37 C.

components.name: Sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.concentration: high-salt (~0.2 M NaCl optimum); exact g/L not in retrievable source
components.evidence: paper: 'high salt (HS) medium buffered with bicarbonate'; Sowers 1984: growth optimum at 0.2 M NaCl

components.name: Magnesium chloride hexahydrate (MgCl2.6H2O) / Magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.evidence: Sowers 1984: maximum growth at 0.05 M MgSO4; HS/Methanosarcina medium contains MgCl2 and MgSO4

components.evidence: M. acetivorans strain C2A was cultured anaerobically with acetate (90 mM) in high salt (HS) medium ... washed with HS ... media minus acetate

sources_chased.what: trace-element + vitamin identity via DSMZ-recommended medium for M. acetivorans DSM 2834 (=Medium 141c)

added: Balch/Wolin's trace mineral solution (11 metals + NTA)

missing_before: Trace metals Al, B, Mo, Ni, Se, W (beyond the Fe/Mn/Co/Zn/Cu already listed)
media.notes: Paper grew M. acetivorans C2A in 'HS medium' (ref 42, Sowers 1984) - a bicarbonate-buffered high-salt Methanosarcina medium. The exact Sowers-1984 gram concentrations could NOT be recovered (that article and the downstream Metcalf 1996/1997 HS-medium papers are scanned with no retrievable full methods). Bulk salts, acetate (90 mM), ferrihydrite (40 mM), methane headspace and N2/CO2 gas phase are directly from the paper's Methods. The trace-element and vitamin solution constituents plus reductants are given from DSMZ Medium 141c, which DSMZ itself recommends for M. acetivorans DSM 2834 (C2A) and which uses the standard Balch/Wolin's mineral + vitamin solutions used across Methanosarcina HS media - hence high element-level confidence but medium confidence on exact concentrations. Acetate (substrate) was omitted for methanotrophic growth; pH 7.2-7.4, 37 C.
